answers: Mind map: central topic in middle, branches for main ideas, sub-branches for details. Visual and non-linear.
Tools: paper + colored pens (best for creativity), or digital: XMind, MindMeister, Miro, Coggle (free).
Benefits: better memory, see connections, organize thoughts, plan projects, and study effectively.
Tips: use colors and images, keep branches short (keywords not sentences), and review/update regularly.
